project_summary:
Correctness patch to bring aitri-reader.js and the alert engine into full compliance with the Aitri ↔ Hub Integration Contract v0.1.63. Fixes six known gaps: missing aitriVersion field, ignored driftPhases fast path, incorrect drift hash logic, wrong artifactsDir default, null projectName fallback, and absent VERSION_MISMATCH alert.

Product Analysis:
  North Star KPI: Zero false-positive or false-negative drift alerts for any project after this patch ships.
  JTBD: 'When I monitor projects that were adopted or initialized before v0.1.20, give me accurate drift and version status without manual workarounds.'
  Guardrail metric: Collection cycle duration for 20 projects must stay ≤5000ms — no regression from current baseline.

  idea_gaps: IDEA.md sections 'Problem' and 'Business Rules' were empty in the main project IDEA.md (not the feature IDEA.md). Feature IDEA.md was complete. No blocking gaps.
no_go_zone: No writes to .aitri or any project file — Hub remains strictly read-only, consistent with the integration contract's fundamental constraint
No aitri adopt --upgrade execution from Hub — Hub does not trigger Aitri CLI commands; it only reads output they produce
No compliance-reader.js implementation — reading 05_PROOF_OF_COMPLIANCE.json is a separate feature not implied by this contract gap
No changes to dashboard.json schema structure beyond exposing the new reader fields (aitriVersion, updatedAt, createdAt) on each project entry
VERSION_MISMATCH is a warning-only alert — Hub does not auto-upgrade projects, prompt to upgrade, or execute aitri commands in response

user_personas.goal: See accurate drift status for projects that were adopted with `aitri adopt` (artifactsDir = '') without false positives
user_personas.pain_point: Hub defaults artifactsDir to 'spec' for all projects, causing artifact lookups to fail on adopted projects and generating spurious drift alerts

user_personas.goal: Know which projects were initialized with an older Aitri version so they can request `aitri adopt --upgrade` before the next pipeline run
user_personas.pain_point: No signal exists today when a project's Aitri version is stale — the team discovers it only when a pipeline command fails unexpectedly

functional_requirements.title: Expose aitriVersion, updatedAt, and createdAt from .aitri schema in readAitriState
functional_requirements.description: readAitriState must extract and return three additional fields from the parsed .aitri file: aitriVersion (string | null), updatedAt (ISO 8601 string | null), and createdAt (ISO 8601 string | null). All three are defensive: return null when the field is absent or not the expected type.
acceptance_criteria: Given a .aitri file with aitriVersion: '0.1.63', when readAitriState is called, then the returned object contains aitriVersion: '0.1.63'
Given a .aitri file without an aitriVersion field, when readAitriState is called, then the returned object contains aitriVersion: null
Given a .aitri file with updatedAt: '2026-03-17T23:00:00.000Z', when readAitriState is called, then updatedAt is '2026-03-17T23:00:00.000Z'
Given a .aitri file without updatedAt, when readAitriState is called, then updatedAt is null
Given a .aitri file with createdAt: '2025-11-01T14:00:00.000Z', when readAitriState is called, then createdAt is '2025-11-01T14:00:00.000Z'

functional_requirements.title: Apply correct defaults for artifactsDir and projectName per the integration contract
functional_requirements.description: readAitriState must return artifactsDir as empty string ('') when the field is absent or an empty string in .aitri — NOT 'spec'. It must return projectName as path.basename(projectDir) when the field is absent or not a string.
acceptance_criteria: Given a .aitri file without an artifactsDir field, when readAitriState is called, then the returned object contains artifactsDir: ''
Given a .aitri file with artifactsDir: '', when readAitriState is called, then the returned object contains artifactsDir: ''
Given a .aitri file with artifactsDir: 'spec', when readAitriState is called, then the returned object contains artifactsDir: 'spec'
Given a .aitri file without a projectName field and projectDir: '/home/user/my-project', when readAitriState is called, then projectName is 'my-project'
Given a .aitri file with projectName: 'aitri-hub', when readAitriState is called, then projectName is 'aitri-hub'

functional_requirements.title: Implement driftPhases fast path in drift detection (v0.1.58+)
functional_requirements.description: detectDrift must check config.driftPhases[] as its first step. If driftPhases is an array and contains any phase key (as string) that matches an approved phase, return true immediately without reading artifact files from disk.
acceptance_criteria: Given a .aitri file with driftPhases: ['2'] and approvedPhases: [1, 2], when detectDrift is called, then it returns true
Given a .aitri file with driftPhases: [] and approvedPhases: [1, 2], when detectDrift is called with the fast path only, then it does not return true from driftPhases alone
Given a .aitri file without a driftPhases field (pre-v0.1.58 project), when detectDrift is called, then it falls through to the hash check without throwing
Given driftPhases: ['ux'] and approvedPhases: ['ux', 1], when detectDrift is called, then it returns true (string phase keys compared with String())

functional_requirements.title: Implement correct dynamic sha256 hash check for drift detection
functional_requirements.description: When driftPhases fast path does not confirm drift, detectDrift must check each approved phase that has a stored hash in artifactHashes: read the corresponding artifact file from disk using ARTIFACT_MAP and artifactsDir, compute its sha256, and compare against the stored hash. A phase with NO stored hash must NOT be considered drifted.
acceptance_criteria: Given approvedPhases: [1], artifactHashes: { '1': '<sha256-of-current-file>' }, and the artifact file unchanged on disk, when detectDrift is called, then it returns false
Given approvedPhases: [1], artifactHashes: { '1': '<old-sha256>' }, and the artifact file modified on disk, when detectDrift is called, then it returns true
Given approvedPhases: [1, 2], artifactHashes: { '1': '<sha256>' } (phase 2 has no hash), when detectDrift is called, then phase 2 is NOT counted as drifted — only phase 1 is evaluated
Given approvedPhases: [1], artifactHashes: { '1': '<sha256>' }, and the artifact file does not exist on disk, when detectDrift is called, then it returns false and does not throw

Given a project with artifactsDir: '', when computing the artifact path for phase 1, then the file is looked up at path.join(projectDir, '01_REQUIREMENTS.json') not path.join(projectDir, 'spec', '01_REQUIREMENTS.json')

functional_requirements.title: VERSION_MISMATCH alert when project aitriVersion differs from installed CLI version
functional_requirements.description: The alert engine must emit a VERSION_MISMATCH warning alert when a project's aitriVersion (from .aitri) differs from the version of the aitri CLI installed globally. The CLI version is obtained by running `aitri --version` once per collection cycle (cached for the cycle). If the CLI is unavailable or the version cannot be parsed, no alert is emitted and collection continues normally.
acceptance_criteria: Given a project with aitriVersion: '0.1.50' and aitri CLI reporting version '0.1.63', when alerts are evaluated, then a VERSION_MISMATCH alert is generated with message containing both versions
Given a project with aitriVersion: '0.1.63' and aitri CLI reporting version '0.1.63', when alerts are evaluated, then no VERSION_MISMATCH alert is generated
Given a project with aitriVersion: null, when alerts are evaluated, then no VERSION_MISMATCH alert is generated
Given aitri CLI is not installed (execFileSync throws ENOENT), when alerts are evaluated, then no VERSION_MISMATCH alert is generated and no crash occurs
Given 20 projects in one collection cycle, when VERSION_MISMATCH is evaluated, then `aitri --version` is called at most once (cached result reused across projects)

user_stories.i_want: to see which Aitri version initialized each project
user_stories.so_that: I can identify projects that need aitri adopt --upgrade before running new pipeline commands

acceptance_criteria.then: the project entry in dashboard.json contains aitriVersion: '0.1.50'

acceptance_criteria.given: a project with .aitri that has no aitriVersion field (pre-v0.1.20 project)
acceptance_criteria.then: the project entry in dashboard.json contains aitriVersion: null and no crash occurs

user_stories.i_want: artifact lookups to use the correct base directory
user_stories.so_that: drift detection and test reader do not fail on projects where artifacts live in the project root rather than spec/

acceptance_criteria.given: a project initialized with aitri adopt (artifactsDir: '' in .aitri)
acceptance_criteria.when: the collector evaluates drift for phase 1
acceptance_criteria.then: it reads 01_REQUIREMENTS.json from path.join(projectDir, '01_REQUIREMENTS.json') — not from spec/

acceptance_criteria.given: a project with no projectName in .aitri at path /Users/dev/finance-tracker
acceptance_criteria.then: the dashboard displays the project name as 'finance-tracker'

user_stories.i_want: drift to be detected immediately from driftPhases without extra file I/O
user_stories.so_that: the collection cycle stays fast when the Aitri CLI has already flagged drift

acceptance_criteria.given: a project with driftPhases: ['3'] set by a previous aitri run-phase call
acceptance_criteria.then: hasDrift is true and no artifact file is read from disk

user_stories.i_want: drift to be detected even when artifacts are modified directly outside the Aitri CLI
user_stories.so_that: I know when someone hand-edited a spec file post-approval

acceptance_criteria.given: phase 2 is approved (artifactHashes has a stored hash for '2') and 02_SYSTEM_DESIGN.md is then edited directly on disk
phase 1 is approved but has no entry in artifactHashes (old project pre-v0.1.51)
acceptance_criteria.then: hasDrift is false — missing hash is not treated as drift

user_stories.i_want: a VERSION_MISMATCH alert when a project's Aitri version is behind the installed CLI
user_stories.so_that: I can proactively run aitri adopt --upgrade on stale projects before pipeline failures occur

acceptance_criteria.given: project aitriVersion is '0.1.50' and installed CLI is '0.1.63'
acceptance_criteria.then: a yellow/warning alert 'Aitri version mismatch: project 0.1.50, CLI 0.1.63' is shown for that project

acceptance_criteria.given: aitri CLI is not installed on the machine
acceptance_criteria.then: no VERSION_MISMATCH alert is generated for any project and the collection cycle completes normally

non_functional_requirements.requirement: sha256 hash computation for drift detection must not cause collection cycle regression
non_functional_requirements.acceptance_criteria: Collection cycle for 20 local projects with all phases approved and artifact files present completes in ≤5000ms — same budget as NFR-001

non_functional_requirements.requirement: aitri --version subprocess failure must not crash or stall the collection cycle
non_functional_requirements.acceptance_criteria: Given execFileSync('aitri', ['--version']) throws any error (ENOENT, timeout, non-zero exit), the collector catches the error, sets installedAitriVersion to null, and completes the cycle within the normal 5000ms budget

non_functional_requirements.requirement: All new logic (FR-010 through FR-014) must be covered by unit tests that run in the existing test suite
non_functional_requirements.acceptance_criteria: Running the existing test command (npm test or equivalent) executes tests covering all 5 new FRs; CI pipeline passes with 0 failures on the main branch after this feature is merged

non_functional_requirements.requirement: Not applicable — this feature adds no new HTTP server or daemon process; existing logging via ~/.aitri-hub/logs/ is sufficient
non_functional_requirements.acceptance_criteria: N/A — no new process; existing NFR-002 error logging covers unreadable projects

non_functional_requirements.requirement: sha256 computation must only read files within registered project directories
non_functional_requirements.acceptance_criteria: Artifact path construction uses ARTIFACT_MAP lookup (whitelist of known filenames) combined with path.join — no user-controlled path segments traverse outside the project directory; path.join cannot produce a path outside projectDir when given a basename from a fixed map

constraints: Node.js built-in crypto module must be used for sha256 — no npm dependencies
execFileSync for aitri --version must use node:child_process built-in — no npm dependencies
All changes are confined to lib/collector/aitri-reader.js and lib/alerts/engine.js — no new files required
technology_preferences: Node.js crypto.createHash('sha256') for artifact hash computation
node:child_process execFileSync for aitri --version with try/catch
